messages.content: You are a Lean 4 autoformalization agent. Generate a small ordered sequence of Lean declarations for the provided LaTeX theorem-like unit. Use only the source text, selector plan, previous-project context if shown, and Lean-checked Mathlib signatures in the prompt. The original aligned Lean declarations, names, statements, and proofs are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "benchmark_policy": {
    "gold_comparison_is_posthoc_only": true,
    "posthoc_alignment_hidden": true,
    "target_lean_available_to_generator": false
  },
  "instructions": [
    "Do not use sorry, admit, placeholders, or comments standing in for proof.",
    "Do not include import statements or markdown fences.",
    "Do not ask for or infer the hidden aligned Lean declaration names/statements/proofs.",
    "Treat selector_unchecked_statement_sketch as non-authoritative mathematical intent only; do not copy its Lean syntax verbatim.",
    "Follow the Lean-checked signatures exactly when they differ from the selector's expected shape.",
    "Use the actual Lean-checked argument order from hydrated_mathlib_context. If the selector expected shape conflicts with the checked signature, the checked signature wins.",
    "If a selected Mathlib API is field-specific, unit-specific, or otherwise not strong enough for the source statement, say so in notes and generate only declarations justified by the visible context.",
    "Do not invent project helper names that are not shown in available_prior_project_context, needed_project_context, or source context.",
    "When available_prior_project_context contains Lean snippets for project definitions or predicates, use those exact names and statement shapes instead of rephrasing the source with raw hypotheses.",
    "Prefer a narrow declaration sequence over a broad bundled conjunction when the source unit decomposes into multiple Lean declarations.",
    "Keep theorem-local assumptions explicit rather than relying on unavailable global variables.",
    "If local_file_context_candidates show useful namespace, open, notation, or variable commands, you may include the needed commands in lean_file_body before the declarations.",
    "If you use a variable command instead of explicit binders, the command itself must appear in lean_file_body. Do not depend on file-scope variables that are not emitted in your output.",
    "Every identifier used in a theorem statement must be introduced by an explicit binder, local declaration, or imported/opened declaration. In particular, source parameters such as coefficient types, matrix sizes, rings, variables, and typeclass assumptions must be bound in the generated theorem, e.g. `{K : Type*} [CommRing K] {n : ℕ}` when the source quantifies over a commutative ring and a natural number.",
    "If you cannot produce a complete proof from visible context, set status to cannot_prove_from_visible_context and leave lean_file_body empty. Never output Lean containing sorry/admit/placeholders."
  ],
  "lean_environment": {
    "default_opens_used_for_context_hydration": [
      "open scoped Polynomial BigOperators",
      "open PowerSeries Finset"
    ],

          "source/project/Mathlib facts actually used"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "task": "Produce Lean declarations for each planned declaration task. The output should be a small Lean file body, without imports, containing the ordered declarations needed for this one source unit.",

                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The matrix with entries x_i * y_j.\n    Label: prop.det.xiyj -/\ndef outerProductMatrix (x y : Fin n → K) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j => x i * y j",

                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Determinant of outer product matrix is 0 for n ≥ 2.\n    Label: prop.det.xiyj -/\ntheorem det_outerProduct_eq_zero (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 2 ≤ n) :\n    (outerProductMatrix x y).det = 0",
                  "line_range": [

                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Second proof of det_outerProduct_eq_zero using matrix factorization.\n    The matrix (xᵢyⱼ) factors as A·B where A has only first column nonzero\n    and B has only first row nonzero. Since n ≥ 2, A has a zero column,\n    so det(A) = 0.\n    Label: prop.det.xiyj (second proof) -/\ntheorem det_outerProduct_eq_zero' (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 2 ≤ n) :\n    (outerProductMatrix x y).det = 0",
                  "line_range": [

                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The matrix with entries x_i + y_j.\n    Label: prop.det.xi+yj -/\ndef sumMatrix (x y : Fin n → K) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j => x i + y j",
                  "line_range": [

                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Determinant of sum matrix is 0 for n ≥ 3.\n    Label: prop.det.xi+yj\n\n    The proof uses matrix factorization: (x_i + y_j) = A * B where A has a zero column. -/\ntheorem det_sumMatrix_eq_zero (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 3 ≤ n) :\n    (sumMatrix x y).det = 0",
                  "line_range": [

                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Second proof of det_sumMatrix_eq_zero using matrix factorization.\n    The matrix (xᵢ + yⱼ) factors as A·B where A has only first two columns\n    nonzero and B has only first two rows nonzero. Since n ≥ 3, A has a\n    zero column, so det(A) = 0.\n    Label: prop.det.xi+yj (second proof) -/\ntheorem det_sumMatrix_eq_zero' (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 3 ≤ n) :\n    (sumMatrix x y).det = 0",
                  "line_range": [

                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The matrix A in the factorization of sumMatrix: first column is x_i, second column is 1, rest are 0.\n    Used in the second proof of prop.det.xi+yj. -/\ndef sumMatrix_factorA (x : Fin n → K) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j =>\n    if j.val = 0 then x i\n    else if j.val = 1 then 1\n    else 0",

            "source_text: \\begin{theorem}[Transposes preserve determinants]\\label{thm.det.transp}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. If $A\\in K^{n\\times n}$ is any $n\\times n$-matrix, then $\\det\\left( A^{T}\\right) =\\det A$."

            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.det.xiyj}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$ be such that $n\\geq2$. Let\n$x_{1},x_{2},\\ldots,x_{n}\\in K$ and $y_{1},y_{2},\\ldots,y_{n}\\in K$. Then,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  x_{i}y_{j}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}\\right)  =0.\n\\]\n\n\\end{proposition}"
          }

            "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.det.xi+yj}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$ be such that $n\\geq3$. Let\n$x_{1},x_{2},\\ldots,x_{n}\\in K$ and $y_{1},y_{2},\\ldots,y_{n}\\in K$. Then,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  x_{i}+y_{j}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}\\right)  =0.\n\\]\n\n\\end{proposition}"

        "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[Transposes preserve determinants]\\label{thm.det.transp}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$.\nIf $A\\in K^{n\\times n}$ is any $n\\times n$-matrix, then $\\det\\left(\nA^{T}\\right)  =\\det A$.\n\\end{theorem}"
